latest.title: Update to Admission of Incentive Shares
latest.content:
Today 15:23
RNS Number : 1810V
Metals Exploration PLC
03 March 2026
METALS EXPLORATION PLC
Update to Admission of Incentive Shares and Total Voting Rights
Metals Exploration plc (AIM: MTL) ("Metals Exploration" or the "Company"), a gold producer, exploration, and development company with assets in the Philippines and Nicaragua, announces that, further to the Company's announcement of 27 February 2026, the admission of 1,182,210 new Ordinary Shares in accordance with the 2025 MIP will now take place at 8.00 a.m. on or around 6 March 2026 ("Admission").
Following Admission, the Company will have 3,279,456,030 Ordinary Shares in issue, of which 299,385,458 Ordinary Shares are held in treasury and, accordingly, the total number of voting rights is 2,980,070,572. Therefore, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculation by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Exercise of Warrants and Options
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6040U
Metals Exploration PLC
27 February 2026
METALS EXPLORATION PLC
Exercise of Warrants and Options, Issue of Incentive Shares and Total Voting Rights
Metals Exploration plc (AIM: MTL) ("Metals Exploration" or the "Company"), a gold producer, exploration, and development company with assets in the Philippines and Nicaragua, announces that it has received notices of exercise in respect of, in aggregate, 3,334,556 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each ("Ordinary Shares") in relation to (i) 2,584,556 warrants with an exercise price of £0.0605 each, raising £156,365.64; and (ii) the exercise of an option for 750,000 Ordinary Shares with an option price of £0.0829 each, raising £62,175.
Issue of Incentive Shares under the Management Incentive Programme ("MIP")
Additionally, the Company has issued a total of 1,182,210 new Ordinary Shares at an issue price of £0.1543 to members of the senior management team, in accordance with the 2025 MIP.
Details of the awards to PDMRs are set out below, with further information being contained in the Appendix to this announcement:

latest.title: Exercise of Warrants, Grant of Options, and TVR
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4812S
Metals Exploration PLC
11 February 2026
METALS EXPLORATION PLC
Exercise of Warrants, Grant of Options, and Total Voting Rights
Metals Exploration PLC (AIM: MTL) ("Metals Exploration" or the "Company"), a gold producer, development and exploration company with assets in the Philippines and Nicaragua, announces that (1) it has received a notice of warrant exercise in respect of 150,000 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each ("Ordinary Shares") with an exercise price of £0.0605 each; and (2) the Company has granted options over 12,000,000 Ordinary Shares to two senior executives, in line with its existing Long-Term Incentive Plan ("LTIP") scheme, as detailed below.
Long-Term Incentive Plan Options ("LTIP Options")
At the General Meeting of shareholders held on 23 August 2024, a LTIP was approved, granting the Directors authority to issue up to 15% of the Company's issued capital as LTIP Options. In accordance with this approval the Company has issued a further 12 million options.
The key terms of these LTIP Options are as follows:
·
Exercise price: £0.0001 per Ordinary Share.
·
Exercise period: 7 years from issue.
Vesting conditions
:
·
A minimum consecutive continuing employment of three years with or, as applicable, a service relationship as a non-executive director, consultant, contractor or other adviser to the Company following each performance condition summarised below having been met. The relevant dates are 31 March 2028 regards to the 7,000,000 LTIP Options and 15 January 2029 regards to 5,000,000 LTIP Options; and
·
One-third of the options shall vest upon the earliest date following the grant date upon which the value of an Ordinary Share (based on the volume-weighted average price over a 30-day period) is at least:
7,000,000
(i) 10 pence; (ii) 15 pence; and (iii) 20 pence.
5,000,000
(i) 20 pence; (ii) 25 pence; and (iii) 30 pence.
Admission and Total Voting Rights
Application has been made to the London Stock Exchange PLC for the admission of 150,000 new Ordinary Shares to trading on AIM ("Admission"), which is expected to take place at 8.00 a.m. on or around 12 February 2026.
Following Admission, the Company will have 3,259,247,368 Ordinary Shares in issue, of which 299,385,458 Ordinary Shares are held in treasury, and, accordingly, the total number of voting rights is 2,959,861,910. Therefore, this figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculation by which they may determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
For further information, please contact or visit:

company_30s.what_they_do: MTL.L is a small, UK‑listed mining and exploration firm. Its main focus is on locating and developing mineral deposits, mainly in the UK and nearby regions, and bringing those resources to market.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a niche of low‑cost, high‑potential exploration. If it discovers a sizable deposit, it could tap into the growing demand for metals used in green technology and electronics, giving it a foothold in a high‑growth sector.
company_30s.current_state: MTL is still in the early stages of its project pipeline, with no significant production yet. It has been issuing and exercising incentive shares and options, but there is no evidence of revenue or a clear path to profitability. The stock trades at a very low valuation, reflecting the uncertainty around its assets.

rns_narrative: The recent filings focus on corporate governance and share‑holding changes—new incentive shares, option exercises, and updates to voting rights. These are routine for a small company but do not signal operational progress.
A February investor presentation and a video update hint at ongoing exploration activities, yet the company has not disclosed any new resource discoveries or drilling results.
The January quarterly update simply reiterates financial and governance data, with no mention of milestones or new funding.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silence with sporadic speculation – the community is largely quiet, with occasional chatter about other mining names.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders hint that MTL could be a hidden gem if it lands a big find, but there is no concrete evidence yet.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most voices express concern about the lack of tangible progress and the company’s heavy reliance on share issuances to stay afloat.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has stayed flat; no new excitement has emerged in the last few days.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no activity for MTL, meaning any news could catch the market off‑guard.

near_term_catalysts: A drilling program that yields a new, sizable ore body.
Securing a funding round or a joint‑venture partnership that injects working capital.
A change in commodity prices that makes the company’s target metals more attractive.
A regulatory approval or a discovery of a high‑grade deposit on the company’s land.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume, a sharp price move above the 200‑day moving average, or a new RNS that announces a resource estimate or financing deal.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock shows no clear signs of a recent capitulation wave; it appears to be in a low‑activity phase. If a catalyst arrives, the mean‑reversion play would likely start within a few weeks to a couple of months.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the data does not yet support a confident contrarian bet.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If MTL announces a significant resource find or a funding round, the price could rally sharply because the market would suddenly see a tangible path to revenue. A 50‑to‑70% upside is conceivable if the new information lifts investor confidence.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without any operational breakthroughs, the company risks remaining in a low‑liquidity, high‑risk position. The worst case is a continued decline as the market forgets the name and the company struggles to raise capital.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside could be several times the current price if a breakthrough happens, but the probability is low. The downside is limited to the current valuation, which is already very low, so the risk/reward ratio is highly asymmetric but also highly uncertain.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a support breakout that is backed by volume, size the position to 5–10% of your discretionary capital, and set a stop just below the recent low to protect against a false break.
playbook.if_watching: Keep an eye on the 200‑day moving average and daily volume. A move of 10% above the average with volume doubling could be a signal to buy.
playbook.if_passing: If the company fails to secure funding or announces a negative resource estimate, consider exiting the position or reducing exposure.

ai_insights.bottom_line: MTL.L is a low‑visibility exploration play; without a clear resource announcement or funding event, the contrarian upside is limited, and any significant move would likely come from a sudden operational breakthrough.
